author: @aldymcpndg14
text: Nice work there sire, anyway i'm wondering if i can go solid state version by employing hall ic and mosfet. The hall ic put in front of the coil and initially activated by the permanent magnet south then energizing the coil hence cancelling the magnetic south effect therefore turning of the hall ic and repeat the cycle again. Any thoughts about it? Thanks

author: @brand4351
text: Hi I am currently trying to achieve magnetic neutralization but am having extreme difficulties I have wrapped the iron core with magnet wire and have put magnets on the iron core but when I power the coil  nothing happens and the magnetic field is not neutralized. Can you please help me figure out what is wrong.

replies.author: @cdiperformanceparts4364
replies.text: You need to over come the magnetic field from the magnets so go higher voltage till you over come the magnetic field created from the magnets........

author: @Buzzhumma
text:
i built a version of this that used gravity and a micro switch. as it came down the micro switch would connect the solenoid and fire it back upetc
it was great

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: That is awesome mate - did you do a video I would be fascinated to see

replies.author: @Buzzhumma
replies.text: Unfortunately that was 30 years ago but very easy to replicate . You change the frequency by the spring tention and or the weight !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @Buzzhumma do you mind if I give it a go?

replies.author: @Buzzhumma
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith sure! Would be great . I used a solenoid from a cassette tape player and a 3 inch nail but you could easily wind your own coil on a sewing machine bobbin!

author: @theAxe6
text:
...There are a lots of videos here on tube about "BI TOROID TRANSFORMER", guys working on this project are claiming that the transformer has "magnetic flux diode properties" so that back EMF does not influence the promary but it's redirected to the second secondary. 
If the flux diode works, it could be used to harvest current produced in the coil as the magnet comes back??

author: @theAxe6
text:
I love your no rotary option, coil from an old dor bell (ding-dong type) would be perfect for generator coil. 
But it's not ok to place expensive magnets to repel each other, they will lose power and eventualy demagnetize due the 50% of the magnetic domains pole shift. 
Neodimiums expected lifetime is about 1000 years while not repelling. 
Maybe better to configure the permanents in attracting mode and reverse coil to repel.
